title: Rich Ethiopian Cheese Bisque
description: A creamy, nutty, and satisfying Ethiopian-inspired bisque made with Vsterbottensost cheese. Perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat the ghee in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and dark golden brown, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the toasted flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to make a light roux.
Gradually add the chicken broth, whisking continuously to prevent lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Use an immersion blender or a regular blender to puree the mixture until smooth.
Return the saucepan to medium heat and add the heavy cream, paprika, and cumin.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the bisque is heated through and the flavors are well combined.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or toasted nuts if desired.
